c74object.name: tap.303~
c74object.description: The tap.303~ object is a monophonic acid bass voice modeled on the Roland TB-303: a sawtooth or square oscillator into the diode-ladder filter (the same kernel as tap.diode~), shaped by the 303's two envelopes — the decay-only Main Envelope sweeping the cutoff (envmod, with the hardware's law: two thirds of the sweep above the cutoff knob, one third below) and the fixed-decay VCA envelope (fast attack, ~1.2 s decay, no sustain, chopped at gate-off). Accented notes hit louder with a faster envelope, scaled by the accent knob — and drive the 303's accent-sweep circuit: a capacitor charges with each accent and pushes the cutoff higher when accents come close together (the famous build-up), scaled by the resonance knob and independent of envmod. Pitch arrives in the left inlet as a MIDI note number (signal, read per sample, or float; fractions are cents). The right inlet is a gate signal whose rising edge triggers the note — edge amplitude encodes accent: 1.0 is a plain note, up to 2.0 fully accented. Slide is legato, exactly like the hardware: change the pitch while the gate is held and it glides through the 303's ~60 ms lag without retriggering — no separate slide input. The note/bang/release messages provide the same control without signals. Envelope constants and the filter's feedback behavior follow measured hardware values (via the Open303 project and the Devil Fish documentation). One voice per object; wrap in mc. for polyphony.

method.name: float
method.description: In the left inlet, sets the pitch (MIDI note number). The next trigger plays it; if the gate is currently held, the voice slides there now.

method.name: note
method.description: Play a note: pitch in MIDI note numbers, accent 0/1, slide 0/1. With slide 1 and a note already sounding, the pitch glides legato instead of retriggering.

method.name: bang
method.description: Retrigger the last note (plain, no accent).

method.name: release
method.description: Release the gate — the VCA chops, like the hardware's gate-off.

method.name: store
method.description: Store the current parameters in a preset slot (1-16).

method.name: recall
method.description: Morph all parameters to a stored preset (1-16). The optional second argument overrides the morph time in milliseconds (default: the interp attribute).

method.name: clear
method.description: Silence the voice and reset all DSP state.

attribute.name: cutoff
attribute.description: Filter cutoff knob in Hz (100-5000; the stock 303 knob spans ~302-2394 — the wider range is the Devil-Fish-style bend). The Main Envelope sweeps around this.

attribute.name: resonance
attribute.description: Filter resonance (0-1.5). Up to 1 is the stock range — squelchy but, like a real 303, never quite self-oscillating; above 1 is the documented bend.

attribute.name: envmod
attribute.description: Envelope modulation (0-1): how much of the Main Envelope reaches the cutoff. Raising it also lowers the sweep's resting point, like the hardware.

attribute.name: decay
attribute.description: Main Envelope decay in ms (200-2000, the stock knob range). Accented notes bypass this and run at ~200 ms.

attribute.name: accent
attribute.description: Accent amount (0-1): how strongly accented notes hit — louder, with a faster envelope, and driving the accent-sweep circuit: consecutive accented notes charge a capacitor that pushes the cutoff higher note over note (the famous build-up, or "wow"), scaled by the resonance knob, draining away during rests. The sweep works even with envmod at zero, like the hardware.

attribute.name: waveform
attribute.description: Oscillator waveform: saw or square (the hardware's two). Switching glides click-free.

attribute.name: solver
attribute.description: Filter nonlinear solver: 0 = fast (default), 1 = exact. See tap.diode~.

attribute.name: interp
attribute.description: Default preset-morph time in milliseconds used by the recall message.

attribute.name: smooth
attribute.description: Anti-zipper interpolation time in milliseconds applied to parameter changes.
